OpenD6 Tools¶

These tools help preparing documents to support the OpenD6 Table-Top Role-Playing Game (TTRPG).

This is focused on two Python libraries that define a Domain Specific Language (DSL) for spells and characters. Each offers a number of helpful features:

  • These modules allow the definition of spells (and miracles) using Python syntax. Since the DSL is declaratory, there’s no real “programming” involved: only adherence to Python syntax rules.

  • Check the math used to compute spell difficulties, and character budgets (dice or character points.)

  • Emit ReStructuredText documents that can be processed by the Sphinx tool (and any other docutils tools) to create published documents.

There are two modules that define the DSL’s:

  • The opend6_tools.magic2 module defines the DSL for designing spells and invocations.

  • The opend6_tools.character module defines a DSL building characters and creatures that fit within a campaign.

Additionally, the following applications are also part of publishing the rule books.

  • The opend6_tools.notebook_extract module extracts Spell or Character definitions from a Jupyter Lab notebook into a Python module.

    This can be converted to RST for publication.

  • The Miscellaneous Computations section describes some modules to compute a few tables in the OpenD6 rules from first principles.

    These include the opend6_tools.die_simplification and opend6_tools.spell_measure modules.

The Notes on DSL Design section describes the overall approach to creating the DSLs.
